basics.name: Lars Benedetto
basics.label: Senior Backend / Platform Engineer
basics.summary: Backend engineer on the Foundation (platform) team at Too Good To Go, owning the core of every Java/Kotlin Spring Boot service in the company. I design the infrastructure other engineers build on — including an annotation-driven SQS messaging stack handling ~100M messages/day across ~1000 queues — and I'm the person who diagnoses the deepest production failures: virtual thread deadlocks, JVM segfaults, and memory issues via heap dump and JFR flamegraph analysis.

work.summary: Too Good To Go is the world's number 1 app for fighting food waste. The Foundation team owns and maintains the core of every Java/Kotlin Spring Boot service across the company.
highlights: Designed and built an SQS message-processing stack with guaranteed at least once delivery now handling ~100M messages/day across ~1000 queues. Engineers add a Java annotation and queue provisioning in AWS, consumers, retries, and scaling are handled automatically. Deep observability with Grafana.

Own the platform other backend engineers build on: distributed caching (Redis), messaging (SNS/SQS), and reusable Spring AOP abstractions that let teams inject complex functionality with a single annotation

Primary responder for production incidents across all JVM services — diagnosed virtual thread deadlocks, tracked down a JVM bug with multithreaded font rendering when using tcmalloc causing production segfaults, and routinely analyze heap dumps and JFR flamegraphs to find memory and performance bottlenecks
Maintain the GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows

work.summary: Too Good To Go is the world's number 1 app for fighting food waste.
highlights: Manage our AWS infrastructure via the Web UI and Terraform
Working with Jenkinsfiles, Helm charts, Kubernetes, ArgoCD, Rancher, etc
Built Apache Camel data pipeline

work.summary: Too Good To Go is the world's number 1 app for fighting food waste.
highlights: Build new features and endpoints for the app and web
Strangle pattern migrate PHP to Spring Boot
Design the core of the TGTG service, the Discover Feed endpoint. A highly flexible system allowing backend engineers to easily add new buckets of items, populated by opensearch.

work.summary: Too Good To Go is the world's number 1 app for fighting food waste.
highlights: Build new features for the Android App
Kotlin, Coroutines

work.summary: Firecracker Software is a United States company that makes word games for iOS, Android, and the Web
highlights: Build the native Android GDPR system for use in all previous and future products
Wrote mini-games in C++ for integration into new game
Collaborate with artists to design UI and UX of new game

work.summary: VIP Traders is a United States company specializing in the import and export of used and slightly-used vehicles and equipment
highlights: Build company website for customers to access company services
Build an Android app for scanning VIN bar-codes into an cloud spreadsheet
Design office automation utilities
Provide general tech support

work.summary: Teach middle school age students to program at summer camp
highlights: Designed a lesson plan for teaching approximately 25 students the basics of programming through AppInventor and Scratch

volunteer.summary: Spokane Public Schools "Reuse before Recycle" program for repairing computers to be distributed to those in need
highlights: Dismantle donated broken computers and assemble the working parts into a computer for redistribution to those in need
